title: size_t to int cast in rx_thread ret comparison
description:
Location: firmware/bridge-zephyr/subsys/lichen/lora_l2.c:210

The comparison uses (int)sizeof(rx_buf) but lora_recv returns int. The comparison is safe here since sizeof(rx_buf) is 255 which fits in int, but the pattern is fragile.

What goes wrong: If LICHEN_LORA_MAX_PHY_PAYLOAD ever exceeds INT_MAX (highly unlikely for LoRa but possible in future refactors), the cast would wrap and the bounds check would be incorrect.

Current code is safe due to k_panic() on overflow, but the cast direction should be reversed: cast ret to size_t after checking ret > 0, then compare to sizeof(rx_buf). This documents intent and is future-proof.
close_reason: Added comment explaining size_t to int cast is safe since LICHEN_LORA_MAX_PHY_PAYLOAD (255) fits in int, and fixed LOG_ERR format to use %d with (int) cast for both values
